benefits.title: Programmes can claim their graduate futures value with confidence
benefits.statement: Programme teams can make stronger, more nuanced and evidence-informed claims about the futures their programme makes possible for students, including distinctive excellence, student pathways, outcomes and value.
benefits.beneficiary: Programme teams, departments, faculties, students, applicants, recruitment teams and King's Edge
benefits.successLooksLike: Programme-level graduate futures stories are confident, nuanced, evidence-linked, academically owned and appropriately caveated. Programme teams recognise the story as their own and can use it in appropriate review, recruitment and student-facing contexts.

enabledBy.outputTitle: First working graduate futures evidence pack

benefits.title: Programmes and faculties can act on graduate futures insight
benefits.statement: Programme teams, departments and faculties can see where existing strengths should be amplified and where curriculum, skills, opportunity, support or articulation work could most strengthen students' futures.
benefits.beneficiary: Programme teams, departments, faculties, Careers and Employability Service, King's Edge and students
benefits.successLooksLike: Programme and faculty conversations identify strengths, questions, support needs and focused opportunities for enhancement without treating the evidence as a deficit report, ranking or judgement on programme quality. Agreed insights enter normal programme or faculty planning and inform King's-level synthesis through 2.4.1.

enabledBy.outputTitle: Practical local actions and faculty learning
